text: As I stand here, the atmosphere feels sterile and immaculate, with glass walls reflecting artificial light in the first image. I feel a sense of anticipation, as if I am on the threshold of a space meant for ideas and innovation but currently empty of interaction. Moving inside, the bold colors and displays of the R+D wall evoke a kind of intellectual curiosity. The words on the screen voice confusion and self-questioning, mirroring my own sense of exposure in this environment dedicated to research and development. There is a quiet tension here—a desire to be understood, and a subtle discomfort with the possibility of being overlooked or misinterpreted. In this space, my thoughts swirl around the often-fraught borderlands between intention and perception.
